message:
fix(db): stop filtering ADD COLUMN migrations in base schema mode and resolve relative DATABASE_URL paths

Schema migrations:
- Remove add_column_migration_is_redundant_for_base_schema filter from
  schema_migrations_base(). Python-imported databases have an older schema
  lacking columns like reaper_exempt and registration_token; filtering
  the ADD COLUMN migrations caused dependent CREATE INDEX migrations to
  fail with "no such column" errors. The migration runner already handles
  "duplicate column name" gracefully via migration_statement_already_satisfied().
- Retain the filter function (dead_code allowed) for potential future use
- Update test assertions: v19_agents_reaper_exempt and v20 columns are
  now included in base mode

Path resolution:
- Add database_url_python_database_candidate() that resolves relative
  paths from DATABASE_URL by trying the absolute candidate when the
  relative path doesn't exist on disk
- Refactor find_python_database into find_python_database_with_database_url
  for testability, with public wrapper that reads DATABASE_URL from env
- search_service.rs sqlite_key_from_database_url now delegates to the
  centralized resolve_search_sqlite_path_from_database_url
- search_v3.rs: consolidate path normalization logic
- Add test for absolute candidate resolution from malformed relative
  DATABASE_URL

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
